subject: Computer Networking
title: How Enterprise Switches Work
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Enterprise Switches Work.

hook.text: A home switch has 8 ports. Enterprise switches have 48 and handle millions of packets per second.

buildup.text: Enterprise switches use ASICs — custom chips built solely for switching. They forward in hardware, not software.

discovery.text: They keep a MAC address table mapping each address to a port. Lookups happen in nanoseconds.

twist.text: Spanning Tree Protocol prevents loops. Without it, a cable in two ports creates a broadcast storm.

climax.text: Stacking lets multiple switches act as one unit. 48 ports become 192 with one management interface.

punchline.text: Enterprise switches move data at wire speed. Zero waste.

quiz.question: Why do enterprise switches use ASICs?
options: To forward packets in hardware at maximum speed
